reasoning: The betting markets heavily favor European teams, with Spain (+400), France (+550), and England (+650) leading the pack. Summing the implied probabilities of all the top European contenders (including Portugal, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Belgium) yields an aggregated implied probability of ~81%. Non-European top contenders like Brazil (+800), Argentina (+800), Uruguay, and the USA yield an implied sum of about 38%. Normalizing out the sportsbook vig (~119% total) gives a "true" combined probability of approximately 68-70% for a European winner. Manifold's own prediction market currently sits at 72%. Given the strong baseline dominance of European teams in recent World Cups (winning 4 of the last 5) and the fact that an expanded 48-team tournament somewhat favors UEFA's overall depth, an estimate of 71% is well-supported. Following rules, probabilities are regressed towards 0.5 for the placeholder extended resolution dates.
